join_order.notes: sub_fk is dense uint32_t index into sub table; use direct array lookup fy2022_bitset[sub_fk] — no hashing needed

load_sub_dimension.description: Load sub columns and build lookup structures

build.name: cik_to_name
build.logic: For each sub row where fy==2022, map cik[i] -> name[i]. Only need one name per cik.

uom_offset_range_scan.description: Use uom_offsets index to get contiguous USD row range

parallel_scan_aggregate.description: Single-pass parallel scan computing SUM(value) GROUP BY cik for both main query and HAVING subquery
parallel_scan_aggregate.logic: For each row i in [usdStart, usdEnd): if !isnan(value[i]) && fy2022_bitset[sub_fk[i]]: cik = sub_cik[sub_fk[i]]; sum_by_cik[cik] += value[i]
parallel_scan_aggregate.notes: Within USD range, rows sorted by sub_fk — good cache locality for sub lookups. Thread-local aggregation maps merged at end.

compute_having_threshold.description: Compute AVG of per-cik sums for HAVING clause

filter_and_topk.description: Filter groups exceeding threshold, partial sort for top 100
filter_and_topk.logic: For each (cik, total): if total > threshold, collect (name=cik_to_name[cik], cik, total). Use std::partial_sort or nth_element for top 100 DESC.

data_structures.fy2022_bitset: bitset(86135) — 1 bit per sub row, ~10.5 KB, fits L1 cache
data_structures.aggregation: flat_hash_map<int32_t, double>(pre_sized=8886) — per-thread, ~200 KB each, fits L2 cache

parallelism.merge: Merge thread-local flat_hash_map<int32_t, double> into global map after scan
parallelism.notes: 33M rows across 64 threads = ~520K rows/thread. Thread-local aggregation avoids contention. sub dimension arrays shared read-only.

aggregation.notes: Same aggregation serves both main query GROUP BY and HAVING subquery. name is functionally dependent on cik — group by cik only, look up name at output.
